name: EMERALD LOOP
address: 1012 SE CLEVELAND SQ LP STE 2, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Front Entry Area; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Establishing multiple access points and ensuring unobstructed paths around the property could improve emergency response actions. By enhancing access opportunities at the rear of the building, responders may experience facilitated movement and flexibility during operations. Additionally, maintaining clear signage and pathways to the front entry area would support efficient navigation and coordination. Prioritizing organized parking in the vehicle area can enhance staging capabilities, allowing for better allocation and movement of emergency vehicles. Managing the landscaped areas conscientiously, such as minimizing potential obstructions near pathways, could further aid visibility and access for responders, thus enhancing overall emergency management effectiveness.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Managing the vegetation directly adjacent to the structure by establishing and maintaining clear separation from the building can enhance defensible space and reduce the chances of fire reaching the structure. Thinning out the scattered trees to create more space between them may help to reduce fuel continuity, allowing for improved fire management options. Maintaining the vegetated perimeter by selectively reducing dense vegetation concentrations could further reduce fuel continuity while preserving some vegetation for aesthetic and ecological benefits. Ensuring that open terrain is maintained without excessive growth or accumulation of combustible materials will support more effective defensible space, potentially facilitating firefighter access and suppression efforts. By focusing on these actions, the property owner may improve resilience to wildfires and enhance long-term property preparedness.
